target.name: The Bot Company
target.founder: Kyle Vogt, Paril Jain, Luke Holoubek

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Robotics & Manufacturing. Range: $1.0B - $1.8B.

private.name: Bedrock Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $1.75B

private.name: Dexterity
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $1.65B

private.name: Kinetic Automation
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B)

data.narrative:
The Bot Company is a Series B robotics and manufacturing company that has raised approximately $550M to date. The valuation analysis draws from a peer set of four same-sector and same/adjacent-stage companies, supplemented by a broader universe of eight private robotics firms and one public comparable (277810.KQ).

Peer-median valuation methodology is the applicable triangulation approach. The four direct comparables—Bedrock Robotics ($1.8B Series B), Dexterity ($1.6B Series B), Hadrian ($1.6B Series C), and Nimble Robotics ($1.0B Series C)—yield a median valuation of $1.6B, with a range of $1.0B to $1.8B. This peer-median approach anchors the central estimate given the stage alignment and sector specificity of the sample.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $1.4B (low) to $1.9B (high), with a median of $1.6B. Key uncertainties include the limited sample size (n=4 for direct peer valuation) and the stage heterogeneity within the peer set, with two comparables at Series C rather than Series B. Capital intensity and unit economics in robotics manufacturing—not present in the provided data—represent material valuation drivers that warrant further scrutiny. The range reflects peer dispersion rather than multiple methodologies, limiting conviction in precision beyond the $1.4B–$1.9B band.
